protein_name: Guanine nucleotide-binding proteins (G proteins) are involved as modulators or transducers in various transmembrane signaling systems. Belongs to the G-alpha family. G(i/o/t/z) subfamily. Note: This description may include information from UniProtKB.

raw_fields.Protein type: Endoplasmic reticulum; G protein, heterotrimeric; G protein, heterotrimeric alpha G((i/o/t/z)); Nuclear envelope

raw_fields.Molecular Function: adenylate cyclase inhibitor activity; G protein-coupled receptor binding; G protein-coupled serotonin receptor binding; G-protein beta/gamma-subunit complex binding; GTP binding; GTPase activity; guanyl nucleotide binding; metal ion binding; nucleotide binding; protein binding
raw_fields.Biological Process: adenylate cyclase-inhibiting G protein-coupled receptor signaling pathway; adenylate cyclase-modulating G protein-coupled receptor signaling pathway; G protein-coupled receptor signaling pathway; negative regulation of insulin secretion; signal transduction
